metadata.title: SPARC Security Scanning and System Integrity

components.title: SPARC Application
components.description: SPARC (Systematic Policy and Regulatory Compliance) is a Rails application for managing NIST 800-53 compliance documentation. This component definition covers vulnerability scanning, flaw remediation, and system integrity controls.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC performs automated vulnerability scanning through multiple tools in the CI security workflow: Trivy filesystem and container image scanning for CVEs in dependencies and base images, CodeQL semantic analysis for code-level vulnerabilities, bundler-audit for Ruby gem advisory checks, and Grype SBOM-driven SCA (#461) that consumes the CycloneDX SBOMs produced by sbom_generation and the Trivy fs/container scans. Grype provides authoritative vulnerability matching against the published SBOM artifacts that downstream consumers receive — the SBOM is scan-verified, not just generated. Scan results are uploaded as SARIF to GitHub Security for centralized tracking; Grype JSON is normalized to HDF via SAF CLI (anchoregrype2hdf). All container image CVE findings are triaged and dispositioned in docs/compliance/sparc-findings.yml with severity classification, deployment-specific rationale, NIST control mapping, and 30-day review cycles.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in .github/workflows/security.yml (Trivy, CodeQL, bundler-audit, grype_sbom_scan). Results uploaded to GitHub Security tab. Container vulnerability baseline: docs/compliance/sparc-findings.yml (76 CVEs triaged, 4 HIGH accepted with mitigating controls, 1 MEDIUM remediated). Grype --fail-on starts at 'critical' for ramp; bumped to 'high' once baseline is triaged.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC supply chain controls: CycloneDX SBOM generation for Ruby gems (cyclonedx-ruby) and container/filesystem (Trivy); known-good version pinning in Gemfile.lock; container image digest pinning. SBOM-driven vulnerability scanning via Grype (#461) means the supply-chain artifacts downstream consumers actually receive are vulnerability-tested before release, closing the gap where SBOMs were generated as evidence but never scanned themselves.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in .github/workflows/security.yml (sbom_generation, trivy_fs_scan, trivy_container_scan, grype_sbom_scan). SBOMs and Grype findings published to the sparc-compliance-latest artifact consumed by sparc-iac.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC verifies component authenticity through Gemfile.lock specific-version pins, container image provenance, and CycloneDX SBOM tracking of component origins. Grype SBOM scanning (#461) augments this by matching each SBOM-declared component against Anchore's vulnerability database — components whose declared identity in the SBOM matches known-vulnerable versions are surfaced as CI failures.

SPARC's runtime CDEF ingestion (#466) treats external authoritative sources as a tracked supply chain. The AWS Labs OSCAL Component Definitions repository (awslabs/oscal-content-for-aws-services) is enumerated via the GitHub Trees API, fetched per-blob with the blob SHA recorded for integrity, and imported only after OSCAL spec-version filtering against SPARC's loaded schemas. Each imported row carries source_url, source_sha, source_commit_sha, and fetched_at provenance fields. The feature is opt-in via SPARC_AWS_LABS_CDEF_ENABLED so air-gapped deployments are unaffected; an ETag-conditional fetch makes the daily refresh near-zero cost when nothing has changed upstream.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in app/services/aws_labs_cdef_import_service.rb + aws_labs_cdef_source_client.rb. License compliance (Apache 2.0 inheritance from upstream): NOTICE + LICENSES/AWS-LABS-OSCAL-CONTENT-LICENSE + docs/compliance/THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md. Read-only + clone-to-amend policy preserves upstream content untouched while letting tenants tailor derivatives.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC addresses flaw remediation through automated vulnerability detection and dependency update tooling. Trivy identifies CVEs in container images and filesystem dependencies. Bundler-audit checks Ruby gems against the Ruby Advisory Database. Dependabot automatically creates pull requests for vulnerable or outdated dependencies. Accepted container findings are documented in docs/compliance/sparc-findings.yml with deployment-agnostic mitigating controls (non-root container, reverse proxy, TLS, network whitelisting, seccomp isolation) and 30-day review cycles for re-evaluation.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in .github/workflows/security.yml and .github/dependabot.yml. Automated PR creation for vulnerable dependencies. Container baseline: docs/compliance/sparc-findings.yml tracks remediation status per CVE.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC implements malicious code protection through Gitleaks secret scanning to detect accidentally committed credentials and secrets, and Trivy container scanning to identify known malware signatures and vulnerable packages in the deployment image. Both tools run automatically in the CI pipeline on every code change. Gitleaks CI scanning includes custom regex rules in .gitleaks.toml that detect leaked SPARC API tokens (sparc_ prefix) and service account tokens (sparc_sa_ prefix), preventing application-specific secrets from reaching the repository.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in .github/workflows/security.yml (Gitleaks, Trivy container scan). Custom Gitleaks rules defined in .gitleaks.toml (sparc-api-token and sparc-service-account-token patterns).

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC supports system monitoring through structured logging to STDOUT in the production environment, enabling integration with external log aggregation services such as AWS CloudWatch. Audit events provide application-level monitoring of security-relevant actions. The production configuration is optimized for centralized log collection and analysis.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in config/environments/production.rb and app/models/audit_event.rb. Logs are CloudWatch-ready.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC receives security alerts through multiple channels: Dependabot alerts for vulnerable Ruby gems and container base image updates, GitHub Security tab aggregation of SARIF-formatted scan results from Trivy and CodeQL, and bundler-audit advisories. These alerts provide timely notification of security-relevant changes affecting the application.

SPARC verifies software integrity through CycloneDX SBOM generation that inventories all software components and their versions. Docker image builds produce digest-verified images, and the CI pipeline ensures that only tested and scanned artifacts are promoted to deployment. The SBOM provides a verifiable record of the software supply chain.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in .github/workflows/security.yml (CycloneDX SBOM, Docker image digests).

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC validates information inputs through multiple layers: Brakeman SAST scanning detects injection and cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities in the codebase, Rails strong parameters enforce allowlisting of permitted request parameters in all controllers, and ActiveRecord validations enforce data type and format constraints at the model level. Avatar upload validates content type against an allowlist (PNG, JPG, GIF, WebP) and enforces a 2 MB file size limit at both client-side (Stimulus controller) and server-side (ProfilesController strong params and User model ActiveStorage validations). Client-side Cropper.js constrains canvas output to 256x256 pixels before upload.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in .github/workflows/security.yml (Brakeman), all controllers (strong params), and models (validations). Avatar input validation in app/controllers/profiles_controller.rb, app/models/user.rb, and app/javascript/controllers/avatar_crop_controller.js. Tested by spec/ and Brakeman scan.
